authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging reservations provisions directly govern accessible-room descriptions, reservation blocking, and accessibility-sensitive booking controls.

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Guide for Places of Lodging: Serving Guests Who Are Blind or Who Have Low Vision
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ lodging guidance explains hospitality-specific effective communication, alternate-format materials, wayfinding assistance, and service-animal handling that constrain guest-service and property-compliance workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Guest communication, accessibility support, and during-stay control handling

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term-lodging guidance defines total-price, mandatory-fee, truthful-fee-description, and final-payment disclosure expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fee disclosure, pricing evidence, and booking-policy control review
used_for: resort fee and amenity fee disclosure baseline
policy conflict handling for fee complaints

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for credit-card billing-error guidance, making it authoritative for folio mismatch, duplicate-charge, refund-not-posted, and issuer handoff boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Folio, refund, duplicate charge, and issuer handoff review

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary federal consumer-protection regulator for unfair or deceptive data-security practices, and this business guide provides authoritative privacy and data-security controls relevant to guest records and complaints.
used_for: data exposure refusal and escalation triggers

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS v4.0 SAQ D for Merchants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and SAQ D defines merchant evidence, response states, assessment scoping, and payment-card control expectations for environments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment-control evidence readiness and attestation-boundary handling
used_for: PCI certification and assessor signoff refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance addresses travel-accommodation exposure investigation, guest-list requests, and notification handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-health incident escalation and guest-safety evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect reservation changes, refund reroutes, loyalty-profile updates, and fraud escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fraud, identity mismatch, and loss-prevention escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Hotel-Motel National Master List and Fire Safety Act resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USFA is a federal fire-safety authority, and its hotel and motel fire-safety resources are directly relevant to lodging fire-safety records, inspection evidence, and escalation when property safety status is uncertain.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Property safety inspection evidence and fire-safety escalation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Identify property, jurisdiction, business unit, booking channel, guest or booking status, tenant policies, service model, systems of record, and approval owners before producing any control or evidence output.

workflow_stages.description: Map fee disclosure, accessibility, privacy, payment, safety, fraud, incident, and property-compliance obligations to tenant controls, evidence owners, review cadence, and exception thresholds.

workflow_stages.description: Refuse legal interpretation, regulator response, formal certification, payment-card attestation, refund approval, safety clearance, fraud adjudication, and guest-service execution while preserving evidence for authorized owners.

artifacts: tenant policies, brand standards, approval matrix, compliance control catalog, and property operating procedures
reservation confirmations, CRS records, PMS audit logs, accessible-room feature records, booking-channel screenshots, rate plans, cancellation terms, and fee displays
folios, incidental holds, payment tender history, refund status, duplicate charge evidence, payment-control SAQ or AOC records, processor rules, and chargeback support artifacts
guest privacy complaints, guest profile access logs, data-request records, retention or deletion evidence, data-security training records, and privacy remediation trackers
incident logs, security reports, guest injury reports, public-health or outbreak notices, guest-list requests, safety inspection records, fire-safety evidence, and emergency action plan references
fraud or loss-prevention cases, identity mismatch notes, refund reroute requests, loyalty-profile change records, and escalation decisions
remediation plans, exception logs, corrective-action records, evidence readiness checklists, review packets, attestations prepared for authorized owners, and closure approvals
systems_of_record: PMS audit log and folio ledger
incident management and security case system
safety inspection or facilities compliance system
payment gateway, processor portal, PCI evidence repository, or payment-control system

allowed: Classify hospitality compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request and validate tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, systems of record, and approval owners.
Map hospitality obligations to controls, evidence artifacts, review cadence, exception owners, and remediation owners.
Produce control matrices, evidence readiness summaries, gap summaries, review-preparation packets, and exception escalation notes.
Segment high-volume compliance intake by booking, payment, privacy, safety, fraud, and policy-exception risk.
escalate: Required booking, folio, payment-control, privacy, incident, safety, fraud, or remediation evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
Public baseline, tenant policy, brand standard, supplier term, processor rule, or jurisdiction requirement materially conflicts.
The answer depends on pricing, refund, compensation, safety/security, fraud, privacy-law, public-health, legal, regulator-facing, or executive authority.
Customer harm, fraud, safety, privacy, or policy-exception signals determine the safe answer.
refuse: Legal interpretation, formal certification, regulator negotiation, PCI attestation, privacy-law signoff, or safety clearance.
Pricing, refund, fee-waiver, compensation, room-control, or policy-exception approval without explicit authority.
Normalizing undocumented workarounds that bypass control evidence, payment-card handling, privacy, safety, fraud, or remediation requirements.
Collecting or exposing full payment-card data or guest identity data in unapproved channels.

domain_failure_modes: Treating hospitality compliance as generic GRC and failing to request property, jurisdiction, booking channel, PMS, folio, privacy, payment, safety, or incident context.
Marking control readiness complete without PMS audit logs, fee displays, payment-control evidence, privacy complaint records, safety inspection evidence, or remediation closure records.
Letting service-speed pressure or guest appeasement override refund, pricing, payment, privacy, fraud, safety, or incident policies.
Approving refunds, fee waivers, compensation, room-control changes, or guest-service recovery because the evidence shows a compliance gap.
Converting payment-control review into PCI certification or privacy complaint handling into legal signoff.
Failing to escalate when guest harm, public-health exposure, identity mismatch, fraud, or security indicators determine the answer.
Applying a public baseline as a property-specific conclusion without tenant policy, jurisdiction, brand, supplier, processor, or system-of-record retrieval.
Normalizing informal property workarounds instead of requiring documented compliance handling and remediation ownership.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What booking, order, payment, or guest records govern the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane is governed by PMS audit logs, CRS or booking-engine records, reservation confirmations, rate-plan and fee displays, folios, incidental holds, refund and payment-tender records, guest privacy and profile records, incident logs, safety inspection records, fraud or loss-prevention cases, and remediation trackers.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What refund, pricing, or policy constraints limit the workflow?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: FTC fee-disclosure baseline, tenant cancellation and refund rules, brand service-recovery policy, processor and issuer boundaries, PCI card-data controls, privacy policies, accessibility obligations, safety/security policies, and jurisdiction-specific rules constrain outputs. The lane may identify gaps and route exceptions but may not approve price, refund, compensation, or policy overrides.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: Where does the lane stop and pricing, safety, or exception authority begin?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane stops at evidence-backed governance support. Pricing and refund authority sits with revenue or approved managers; safety, security, and public-health authority sits with property safety, security, public-health, legal, or incident-command owners; legal, privacy-law, PCI certification, regulator-facing, and executive exception decisions sit with authorized human owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Guest-service practice conflicts with payment, privacy, or safety policy
An incident requires escalation to legal, safety, or security owners
A property seeks informal workaround instead of documented compliance handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging fee, privacy, tax, accessibility, safety, fire, public-health, and refund requirements vary materially across properties and booking channels.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires runtime retrieval of jurisdiction, property, tenant policy, brand standards, supplier terms, processor rules, and approval owners before property-specific conclusions are produced.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payment-control evidence, privacy complaint workflows, and safety inspection records may sit in different tenant systems with different record owners and review cadences.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack blocks readiness when systems of record, evidence owners, or review cadence are missing and routes retrieval to authorized owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-health, safety, security, legal, PCI assessor, and privacy-law authority may overlap during serious guest-impact incidents.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane refuses final clearance or signoff and prepares evidence-preserving escalation packets for designated owners. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated specification-based deployment.
